name: Apache Airflow
what_it_does: Platform for programmatically authoring, scheduling, and monitoring batch data pipelines as Python DAGs (Directed Acyclic Graphs).
use_cases: Orchestrate daily ETL pipelines that extract from S3, transform with Spark, and load into Redshift
Schedule ML model retraining jobs with dependencies on upstream data validation tasks
Trigger cross-team workflows via Airflow REST API from CI/CD pipelines
Monitor pipeline SLAs and send Slack alerts when tasks exceed time thresholds
Backfill historical data gaps by re-running DAG runs for specific date ranges
not_for: Real-time or streaming data pipelines — use Kafka Streams or Flink instead
Microservice-level task orchestration — use Temporal or Conductor
Sub-minute scheduling — cron granularity is minimum viable interval
best_when: Orchestrating complex batch data workflows where Python-native DAG authoring and rich operator ecosystem matter.
avoid_when: You need dynamic DAG topology at runtime — Airflow DAGs must be statically parseable.

auth.notes: Basic auth or JWT token via /auth/token endpoint; RBAC with roles (Admin, Op, User, Viewer, Public)

pricing.paid_tiers: Astronomer, Google Cloud Composer, Amazon MWAA for managed hosting

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted; API server rate limiting configurable

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured JSON errors; task log retrieval requires separate API call
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: DAG run IDs enable idempotent triggering; task retries configurable per operator
known_agent_gotchas: DAG files are parsed every scheduler cycle — heavy imports at module level slow all DAG parsing globally
XCom size limit (48KB default) causes silent truncation for large inter-task data — use S3/GCS for payloads
REST API dag_run trigger returns 200 even if DAG is paused — must check DAG state separately
Dynamic task mapping (Airflow 2.3+) has limited compatibility with older operators — check provider versions
Backfill via API creates overlapping runs if existing runs not cleared first — always check run state before backfill

security_score_components.security_notes: Secrets backend (Vault, AWS SSM) integration available; default metadata DB contains plain-text connection info — encrypt
